idea.hook: How long would it take to reach the nearest star?
facts: The nearest star to our Sun is Proxima Centauri, 4.24 light-years (or ~40 trillion km) away.
The fastest human-made object ever recorded is NASA's Parker Solar Probe, which can reach speeds of 690,000 km/h (430,000 mph).
At that record-breaking speed, how long would it take to reach Proxima Centauri?
idea.payoff: It would still take over 6,600 years to get there! Our cosmic neighborhood is truly vast.
idea.title: Journey to the Nearest Star: How Long?! 🚀🌌

scenes.text: How long to reach the nearest star?
scenes.visual: A vast, dark space background with subtle distant stars twinkling. A single, bright star might appear briefly in the distance.

scenes.text: Nearest star: Proxima Centauri, 4.24 light-years (40 trillion km) away.
scenes.visual: A graphic depicting two stars: one representing our Sun (yellow-white) and a smaller, reddish star representing Proxima Centauri, with a curved dotted line indicating the vast distance between them.

scenes.text: Fastest ever: NASA's Parker Solar Probe at 690,000 km/h!
scenes.visual: A sleek, stylized animation of the Parker Solar Probe speeding across the screen with motion lines, against a backdrop of distant stars.

scenes.text: At that speed, how long to Proxima Centauri?
scenes.visual: A conceptual timeline or progress bar. A small indicator icon (like a tiny spaceship) rapidly moves along a very long line, representing the journey, with a large, animated question mark appearing prominently.

scenes.text: Over 6,600 YEARS! Our cosmic neighborhood is truly VAST.
scenes.visual: An awe-inspiring shot of a spiral galaxy or a dense star field, emphasizing the immense scale of space. The text appears dynamically, with '6,600 YEARS!' being very prominent.
